summary: Full re-test after switch from Emergent Google OAuth → classic email/password JWT admin (TTL 12h, localStorage 'fives_admin_token', Bearer header). Backend: 25/25 pytest tests pass — covers POST /api/auth/login (success returns {token,user}, wrong password=401 with detail 'Email ou mot de passe incorrect', unknown email=401), GET /api/auth/me (no header=401, bad token=401, valid Bearer returns role=admin and excludes password_hash), public GET /api/players + /api/matches + /api/stats/global + /api/stats/players + /api/stats/player/{id} + POST /api/team-generator without any Authorization header, all six admin writes (POST/PATCH/DELETE players & matches) return 401 without token and 200 with admin Bearer (Create→GET persistence verified for players + matches; ELO unchanged from prior iteration), and seed_admin() idempotency verified by restarting backend and re-logging in successfully. Frontend: Found CRITICAL JS crash on /players page ('isAdmin is not defined') — fixed by importing useAuth + destructuring isAdmin in Players.jsx. After fix: Login page renders with login-email-input/login-password-input/login-submit-btn; wrong creds show login-error 'Email ou mot de passe incorrect'; anonymous user can navigate /, /players, /matches, /generator (no redirect), and admin-only buttons (add-player-form, edit-player-*, delete-player-*, header-new-match-btn, edit-match-*, delete-match-*, new-match-btn) are hidden; /matches/new redirects anon to /login; after admin login the JWT is stored in localStorage under 'fives_admin_token', header shows logout-btn, admin buttons appear (form=1, rows=4, edit=4, delete=4 on /players; header-new-match-btn=1 + edit/delete on /matches; submit-match-btn=1 on /matches/new); logout clears token, hides admin buttons, and /matches/new redirects again.

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError 'isAdmin is not defined' caused the page to crash on render for BOTH anonymous and admin users — page rendered blank. Root cause: file referenced `isAdmin` 7 times but never imported useAuth() nor destructured it. FIXED in this iteration by adding `import { useAuth } from "@/context/AuthContext";` and `const { isAdmin } = useAuth();` at top of Players component.

action_items: Review the /app/frontend/src/pages/Players.jsx fix applied by testing agent (added useAuth import + destructure). All other isAdmin consumers (Dashboard, Matches, Login) already had correct imports.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py CORS uses allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True (line ~469). With Bearer header auth this is harmless but browsers still reject credentialed wildcard CORS — tighten to explicit frontend origin from .env.

server.py response_model on /api/players POST/PATCH expects Player.joined_at as datetime, but code stores `joined_at` as ISO string in Mongo and converts back on each read. Consider storing BSON Date for native sort and simpler model handling.
server.py /api/matches PATCH does NOT validate that team players still exist via _ensure_players_exist if only score is changed — it does (merged + _ensure_players_exist). OK.
Frontend ProtectedRoute redirects to /login while loading=true displays 'Loading…' fallback — good UX, prevents flash redirect on hard refresh.

axios response interceptor in lib/api.js clears token on ANY 401 — including 401 from /api/auth/login attempts with wrong password. This isn't harmful (no token existed) but consider scoping the interceptor to skip /auth/login responses to avoid unintended side effects in future flows.
JWT_SECRET is committed to backend/.env in the repo. Acceptable for preview env but must be rotated for prod.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (rewrote for JWT Bearer auth — 25 tests across TestAuth/TestPublicReads/TestWriteUnauthorized/TestAdminCRUD/TestSeedIdempotent)
/app/frontend/src/pages/Players.jsx (added missing `useAuth` import and `const { isAdmin } = useAuth()` to fix runtime ReferenceError)
test_credentials: Admin: qenquen@hotmail.com / 130588 (seeded idempotently from backend/.env on every startup). JWT obtained via POST /api/auth/login, stored client-side under localStorage key 'fives_admin_token'.
seed_data_creation: Pre-existing 4 players (Alice, Bob, Carlos, Diana) and 1 match retained. Backend tests create TEST_* players and one match, then clean up in test_delete_players_cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth model = JWT Bearer (no cookies). Obtain token via POST /api/auth/login with {email,password}; send as 'Authorization: Bearer <tok>'. Public GETs need NO header. localStorage key in frontend = 'fives_admin_token'. Backend tests: cd /app && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> python -m pytest backend/tests/test_backend.py. Frontend uses /generator (not /team-generator). After login, redirect to '/'. The Players page had a missing useAuth import that was fixed this iteration — verify the fix is still present.
rca of the issue: Players.jsx referenced `isAdmin` in 7 places (lines 106, 167, 172, 174, 196, 217) but did not import useAuth() nor destructure isAdmin. When React rendered the component, ReferenceError was thrown on the first conditional `{isAdmin && (...)}`, crashing the whole page (caught by React's default error boundary, leaving the route empty). Reproduction: navigate to https://elo-kickoff.preview.emergentagent.com/players as any user (anon or admin) → page is blank, console shows 'PAGEERROR: isAdmin is not defined'. Mitigation applied: added `import { useAuth } from "@/context/AuthContext";` and `const { isAdmin } = useAuth();` at the top of the Players component.
